Apex Legends is the only game where:

- you loot for 20 minutes  
- become emotionally attached to a purple shield  
- and then get deleted in 0.3 seconds by a bald Wraith with 40,000 kills and no social life.

My random teammates move like NPCs from a mobile game:
one lands alone,
one disconnects,
and the third guy is fighting an entire squad with a Mozambique and pure confidence.

The movement is amazing.
The gunplay is incredible.
My mental health has been sent to another dimension.

“Last game before sleep” is the biggest lie ever told.

10/10.
Installed it again 14 minutes after uninstalling.

高一入坑的这个游戏，那时同学都说有个游戏超级好玩，也是我第一个fps游戏。到了高三，那时候爸妈不让玩电脑，我就一直惦记着这个游戏。别的不说apex确实是我心里最好玩的fps，就算ea服务器动不动就炸了，就算重生平衡做的一坨，每次有人找我玩，我也都会上线打上两把。真的算是我的fps白月光了。

Sim, depois de quase 2000h ainda não recomendo, especialmente se você não quer se estressar.
Apex já foi muito bom, mas atualmente a situação está desinteressante. 

Se você é brasileiro ou está na América do Sul, esqueça jogar no servidor daqui, só tem cheaters, cronus zen e jogadores que não veem luz solar há dias; além de ter que esperar mais de 10 min pra achar UMA PARTIDA. Então sim, se quiser ter o mínimo de diversão possível, vai ter que jogar lagado, em server dos EUA com 150 de ping (em dias bons).

Não há matchmaking nas partidas, por mais que tentem dizer que sim. São ínfimos os ajustes no battle royale e zero, literalmente zero em partidas de "MIXTAPE" (dominação, corrida de armas e mata mata). O problema se agrava nesse modo MIXTAPE, pois como não há pareamento equilibrado, você acaba jogando com players muito acima de você, com habilidades muito melhores.

O sistema de denuncias do Apex é falho e praticamente inútil. Existem milhares de jogadores com contas secundarias (smurfs), usando cronus zen ou hacks, e não há nenhum feedback ou retorno sobre suas denuncias, não acontece nada, NADA, com os players.
-Presenciei um player level ~500 com simplesmente 5300 kills (sendo que a maioria dos players CASUAIS tem por volta de 7000 kills no level 1100-1300). 
É algo que sinceramente não gera prazer nenhum em jogar.

Apex Legends é da EA e isso já é um problema por si só.
Assim como sua imagem, o Apex possui monetização agressiva, são skins com mira e formato melhores, pacotes caros, moeda cara, customização de skins bloqueada se não possuir a que é paga...
-E houve recentemente a ideia e realização (algo que voltaram atras por causa das criticas) de ter que recomprar o passe cada temporada por 110 reais.
Não há problema nisso se o jogo já houvesse apresentado esse sistema desde o dia 1, mas foi uma mudança abrupta depois de varias temporadas (21 temporadas) somente no pensamento de lucrar.

As áreas dos mapas mudam toda temporada (mudar no sentido de rework) e isso gera uma desconexão e necessidade de se acostumar gigantesca. Existem mapas que não funcionam nas partidas ranqueadas e outros que são desnecessariamente grandes para partidas rápidas.

Não se pode escolher o mapa que vai jogar, é o que está disponível e ainda rotaciona entre 3, ou seja, jogue com o que estiver lá, e isso relaciona o dito acima dos mapas certos pra cada tipo de jogo.

No quesito de novas lendas (personagens jogáveis), é a mesma ideia do Valorant: Lançar o boneco quebrado e muito forte e só arrumar em datas especificas ou no final da temporada.

Além do pensamento de que se uma lenda é muito pickada (usada) ela está forte e precisa ser nerfada, o problema é que nem sempre isso é verdade.
-Muitas vezes não é que uma lenda muito jogada esteja forte mas sim que as outras estão fracas; no entanto, isso nem se passa pela cabeça da Respawn (estúdio que desenvolve o Apex Legends).
Isso faz com que muitas lendas boas caiam no esquecimento porque tomaram um nerf injusto ou sejam esquecidas por serem fracas demais.

São muitos pontos que tiram sua diversão nesse jogo, ou te irritam bastante.

Управление лаконичное и понятное даже новичкам: к примеру, автоматическая установка модификаций оружия и снаряжения позволяет сохранять высокую динамику матчей. Система меток и сообщений — отдельный разговор: такого простого и приятного в использовании средства коммуникации со случайными союзниками я ещё не встречал.

Acho que Apex Legends foi o battle royale mais desbalanceado que eu já joguei na minha vida. O matchmaking não tem nenhum critério a ponto de você ficar por um bom tempo trocando tiro com algum squad pra chegar algum viciado ou hacker que não tem nada haver com a troca e simplesmente matar ambos com extrema facilidade.

Não consegui entender muito bem mas aparentemente é "permitido" macro e se utilizar de alguns bugs pra movimentação, nas primeiras horas o meu contato com esses jogadores foi o equivalente a um ser pré-histórico descobrindo o fogo: eu simplesmente não entendi NADA.

Da pra ganhar no tiro mas isso não é o suficiente, tem o kit de armas do meta que basicamente 99% do servidor vai usar. Eu tentei não seguir esses padrões e virei um café com leite no squad enquanto esperava meus amigos me carregarem.

強い敵にやられた、プレデターにひき殺されたやられたと思ったら9割PAD。やられた後の観戦での視点の動きデスボの漁り方でわかる。とりあえずエイムアシストを弱くしてくれないと面白くない。キーマウの努力が報われない。PADのほうが人口が多いのおかしいと思った方がいい。半々になる努力をしてください。ていうかエイムアシストじゃなくてキャラコンアシストでいいんじゃないですか？キーマウの優位なところはキャラクターの操作性にあると思います。なのでPADでそれをカバーできるようにキャラコンにアシストをすればいい。まんぶーはPAD専用にするとか。
そもそもPC版なんだから、キーマウでやるのが普通になるべき。PADでやりたいのならPS4や5のサーバーでやればいい。PC版のゲームを順当にキーマウでプレーしてる方が淘汰されてるのはおかしすぎる。うまいPADの方はエイムアシストのない、スモーク中でも 当ててくる。エイムアシストがなくても敵を倒せる事実がある時点でアシストはいらない。アシストがないほうがPADプレイヤーの差別化もできていいと思う。今は、エイムアシストのせいで、みんな当てすぎ。戦ってる最中にPADってわかるような当て感。おかしい。まじで。PADのほうが多いからあんまり問題にならんかもしれんがもとはPCのゲーム、PADのエイムアシストをどうにかしろ。あと、大会とか見ててもPADの視点には華がない。
あとPS4や5の方たちのエイムアシストが強い。おかしい。PC鯖に来てるのだから、チャレンジャーとしてもっと弱くあるべき。運営はバランスをとれてると思ってるかもしれないが全く取れていない。
あともう一つ、マッチングシステムがおかしいプラチナ部隊とプレ部隊がマッチングしてるのを見かけるが、本来パーティーで組めないランク差があるのにマッチングで敵にプレが来るプラチナがあまりにもかわいそう。あと、何回も繰り返して申し訳ないが、ダイヤ3、4でプレとマッチングすんのもやめろ。

この少数派の意見は、どうにもならないだろうが、愚痴を書かせてもらった。ここまで読んでくれた方がいたら感謝します。マッチングの合間に書いたので、文章ぐちゃぐちゃですが許してください。

Ich hab Apex Legends jahrelang nicht mehr angefasst. Nach den ersten paar Seasons war irgendwie die Luft raus, und ich bin zu anderen Shootern gewandert. Aber die ganzen Gerüchte, dass das Spiel "tot" sei, haben mich dann doch neugierig gemacht – also hab ich es nochmal installiert. Und was soll ich sagen? Das Ding lebt. Wirklich.

Der neue Legend ist der erste neue Charakter seit einer Weile – und der macht richtig Laune. Ein Skirmisher mit Rollen an den Beinen, der einfach nur schnell ist. Seine Fähigkeiten passen perfekt zu einem aggressiven Spielstil. High risk, high reward – genau mein Ding.

Was mich früher immer genervt hat, waren die Momente, wenn ein Teammitglied stirbt und man ewig zum Beacon rennen musste. Das ist jetzt Geschichte. Es gibt einen Deathbox-Respawn – du kannst deine Kumpels direkt da wiederbeleben, wo sie gefallen sind. Das Game ist einfach schneller, flüssiger, weniger totes Herumstehen. Das merkt man in jedem Match.

Technisch läuft es bei mir jetzt butterweich. Früher hatte Apex auf manchen Systemen seltsame Probleme – die sind weg. Die Optik wurde aufpoliert, bessere Beleuchtung, flüssigere Schatten. Insgesamt einfach runder als früher.

Ganz eitel Sonnenschein ist es aber nicht. Gerade die Hardcore-Spieler meckern viel. Sie sagen, dass die Entwickler oft Änderungen einführen, die keiner versteht, und das Feedback der Community nicht richtig umgesetzt wird. Besonders im Ranked-Bereich fühlen sich viele im Stich gelassen – das Matchmaking ist nicht immer fair, und Server-Probleme gibt es auch ab und zu. Auch Cheater sind leider nie ganz verschwunden.

Wenn du, wie ich, das Spiel mal aus den Augen verloren hast, ist jetzt ein guter Zeitpunkt zurückzukehren. Es fühlt sich frisch an, die neuen Inhalte machen Laune, und die technischen Verbesserungen sind spürbar. Klar, für absolute Competitive-Hardcore-Spieler mag da noch Luft nach oben sein, aber für alle anderen ist Apex Legends gerade in einer guten Verfassung.

Mit Freunden im Team macht es einfach süchtig – dieses "eine Runde noch, ich schwör, es ist die letzte"-Gefühl ist zurück. Wer einen actionreichen, taktischen Shooter sucht und Bock auf schnelle Baller-Action hat, sollte mal reinschauen. Der Zug ist noch lange nicht abgefahren.

This game has gripped my life since its release back in 2019. For a few years there, this was the only game I was playing on my Xbox. 

I will say, respawn truly has made something special here. The gunplay, the movement, the abilities, and even the sound when you knock someone hits like crack. It’s so satisfying, and I haven’t been able to find another game that compares. 

That being said, this game has issues. My main gripes with Apex include:

1. This game brings out the worst in humanity. Apex has an extremely toxic player base that act like their life depends on winning a match. No one has compassion for each other.

2. Ranked has never been balanced properly, and the Devs do not listen to what their community wants.

3. This game is not fun to play without friends. Apex is not friendly to new players and has a steep learning curve, so anyone you try to introduce to this game is cooked.

Apex Legends est un jeu très stimulant grâce à son gameplay rapide et dynamique. Chaque personnage possède des capacités uniques, ce qui rend les parties stratégiques et amusantes. Le jeu offre aussi de très beaux graphismes et une excellente fluidité. Le travail en équipe est important, ce qui encourage la communication entre les joueurs. Les mises à jour régulières ajoutent de nouveaux contenus et maintiennent l’intérêt de la communauté. Enfin, le système de déplacement, comme les glissades et l’escalade, rend l’expérience très immersive et originale. pur plaisir et dopamine et de rage parfois.

Over 1500 hours with a max of 400 hours of enjoyment. I still play this game because there are only 2 relevant battle royale games: this and Skyscraper Building Simulator, where you need to own a server in your house to take a wall from an unemployed dropout with no purpose in life.

The good things about this game:

1. ABSOLUTELY NOTHING (for now, I guess).
2. In the past, the permanent Arena mode and the occasional Solo mode were the only game modes that I enjoyed playing.

Let's start with the truth:
1. The servers are ass.
2. Half the players are using Zen, configs, scripts, etc. (they are mostly common in LTMs and Ranked).
3. You can have over 4k hours and play Ranked in Diamond lobbies, and this game still manages to put on your team 2 level 50 kids or some mentally ill stupid-ass human whose only purpose in life is to ragebait you.
4. The map rotation makes no sense.
5. The Ranked system makes no sense (they punish solo queue Diamond players and reward 3-stacking Preds).
6. CONSOLE PLAYERS!!!

In conclusion? Just drink a few shots of vodka with beers before you hop into a Ranked match just to annihilate any sense of regret that appears after a maximum of 5 minutes of playing this miraculous game.

Apex Legends ranked mode has become one of the most frustrating competitive experiences I’ve played.

The rank reset system is terrible. Apex Predators, the highest rank in the game, get pushed all the way down to Gold after resets. Because of that, Masters and Diamonds get pushed into Silver and lower ranks too. Regular players end up fighting people way above their actual skill level just because of this terrible reset design. Ranked is supposed to separate players by skill, not throw everyone together at the start of every split.

The party rank system is also easily abused. Apex Predators can queue with lower-ranked Diamond players and get into easier lobbies than they should. The solution is simple: matchmaking should always be based on the highest-ranked player in the squad. Almost every competitive game already understands this.

Audio is another massive issue that somehow still isn’t fixed. Enemy footsteps randomly disappear even when players are extremely close. It’s honestly embarrassing that a multi-million dollar company still has audio problems like this after so many years. The recent update made it even worse because Octane, who should have loud footsteps due to his speed, is now strangely quiet.

And that’s without even talking about controller dominance becoming the norm. Aim assist is so strong that it removes much of the difficulty of recoil control and close-range tracking, making fights feel less skill-based for mouse and keyboard players.

The movement and gunplay are still great, but ranked integrity, broken audio, and questionable balancing decisions continue to drag the game down.

Juego bastante a los Battle Royale y uno de los que más horas le metí fue Apex Legends. Entre Steam y Origin debo tener unas 300 horas aproximadamente, y sinceramente puedo decir que fue uno de los juegos que más me costó aprender a jugar “bien”.

A diferencia de otros Battle Royale como Warzone, PUBG o incluso Counter cuando hablamos de shooters competitivos, Apex tiene una movilidad y un gameplay muchísimo más rápido y complicado. El tema de las habilidades, el movimiento, el tiempo para matar y las peleas súper frenéticas hacen que sea bastante difícil dominarlo. De hecho, es de esos juegos que, aunque le metas muchísimas horas, sentís que nunca terminás de aprender del todo. Personalmente es uno de los shooters que jamás pude llegar a dominar aunque sea un poco, y justamente eso también hace que tenga algo especial.

Algo que sí siento es que Apex es un juego prácticamente hecho para jugar con amigos. Obviamente podés jugar solo, pero la experiencia cambia muchísimo cuando entrás con squad y buena comunicación. No es como CS, PUBG o Warzone donde capaz podés carrear más individualmente o jugar tranquilo solo; acá el trabajo en equipo y coordinar habilidades es demasiado importante.

Dentro de los Battle Royale, Apex sigue siendo bastante único comparado a los demás. La movilidad, los personajes y el ritmo de las partidas hacen que se sienta totalmente distinto. Pero también tiene varios puntos negativos. Uno bastante molesto es el hecho de no poder jugar realmente solo dentro del modo principal, porque dependés muchísimo del squad y muchas veces te terminan tocando compañeros que ni hablan o se van apenas los matan.

Y otro problema grande es que hoy en día la mayoría de jugadores son demasiado buenos. Se nota muchísimo que ya casi no hay jugadores nuevos, entonces muchas partidas terminan siendo bastante complicadas incluso para gente que ya tiene horas encima. A veces parece que todos juegan como si estuvieran en ranked competitivo y eso puede hacer que aprender o simplemente relajarse jugando sea medio frustrante.

Aun así, Apex me parece un juegazo y probablemente uno de los Battle Royale más originales que salieron en los últimos años. Tiene una jugabilidad increíble, peleas súper intensas y momentos que posta son muy divertidos cuando jugás con amigos. Es difícil, sí, pero justamente eso también hace que cuando una partida sale bien se sienta muchísimo más satisfactoria.

I've played Apex Legends on the PS4 way back when it was still in Season 0 and I quit playing at around Season 4. I've recently picked up the game again in hopes it might've picked itself back up, but no, the game fell off. 

The gameplay mechanics feel like they're throwing things at the wall and hoping something sticks, the gunplay feels off from how I remembered it, and I don't even have some of the legends I used to have even after linking accounts (I think I'm missing Revenant and another legend). Every match feels like a drag and the ranking system feels tacked on and forgotten about.

The community is very toxic too. I was trash talked for not having Newcastle as a returning player and a player insulted my mother after he died far away from me and another teammate and I died trying to get his banner.

Overall, it's not worth coming back to no matter how much money you've spent in the past. If you want to play a game like Apex, just play Farlight instead. The game has less players and the lobbies can feel harder, but the gunplay feels much better and the abilities feel more fun to learn and play around. 

TLDR: Apex bad because bad gameplay and bad community. Play Farlight instead.

I've played this game quite a lot for a mostly-casual player (probably around double what the steam playtime shows since I started on the EA client all the way back when Apex was brand new, I remember Octane and Wattson coming out, I've *been* been here), the fact that almost 2 years ago now they put a blanket blacklist on all Linux players to "prevent cheating" and it's still in effect to this day is what drove the decision to leave a negative review. I've dual-booted for a while, still played occasionally after the Linux exclusion and the number of cheaters has not lessened as far as you can tell while playing. But what did lessen? Number of overall Windows users. And yet, the blanket ban still remains. I don't use Windows to game anymore. A lot of Linux players never did. And a lot of us have spent a lot of time playing this game prior to October 2024. We should not be forced to choose between forfeiting whatever reason a given Linux user has for using their OS or forfeiting a game we used to enjoy. Initially there was still hope maybe once they're proven wrong, they'd revert their decision, but that does not seem likely by now. I don't know if they're just allergic to admitting they made a bad choice, but this blacklist is such a weird hill for Respawn to die on, especially with the success of the SteamDeck and now with the Steam Machine on the horizon, you'd think they'd at the very least care more about securing these players.

Apex Legends es, entretenido, un juego que ya ha tenido sus arreglos, aunque aun faltan algunas cosas por solucionar, el sistema de match, los jugadores trolls y toxicos.
Lamentablemente no es muy amigable para los jugadores casuales, ni novatos, si eres nuevo o buscas una experiencia relajada, te vas a encontrar con un problema de habilidad. No es muy amigable empezando y el sistema de match no ayuda, al igual que el serio problema de smurfs y hackers que tiene.
* Toxicidad persistente: Existen un problema serio con la comunidad que el estudio aun no logra resolver y ellos mismos admiten si les envias un correo o un reporte sobre algun jugador siendo toxico, o con un mal comportamiento en el voice chat, al igual que si respondes por el canal de texto, se puede malinterpretar y recibir el reporte por que no revisan el voice chat donde la mayoría de jugadores tóxicos aprovechan para insultar y esperar que les respondas, igualmente es un vacío legal que la propia dev confirma que no les interesa revisar, dejando vía libre a que flamen, en pocas palabras el vice chat se diría como "tierra de nadie". Al no estar vigilado de la misma forma, los jugadores smurf, al igual que trolls, solamente hostigaran y insultaran sin miedo a represalias. Ya parte de ellos saben que el sistema no registra el voice chat, a menos que lo grabes y envíes, pero seguramente un bot automatizado lo atenderá y no llegara a nada, a si generándose un ambiente toxico del cual se aprovechan constantemente. Realmente no merece la pena tener el voice chat activo, el juego cuenta con un sistema de comunicación lo suficientemente completo para dar a entender acciones.
Si eres nuevo, te recomiendo silenciar/mutear a tu equipo, al igual que si ya tienes tiempo jugando y vuelves a reincorporarte sin saber esto de ante mano. No lo recomiendo para jugadores que buscan una experiencia competitiva sana o casual.

I remember when i first downloaded apex back in S3 when one of my cousins told me about it. Immediately i was all in the gladiator esk like vibe, the grounded more slow paced version of Titanfall's gameplay, the legends, the guns, the story. I feel bad for games like Apex man at it's core on paper it is a phenomenal and pretty darn good game. I can see why some people come back after a while. But i can also see why a lot of people talk straight bad about it and some of those reasons are most definitely the reasons why i left. I'd say my top 3 are the matchmaking, EA, and the live service model which in all honesty is holding a lot these games back to be honest. I mean how good would be Apex be if it wasn't a live service game, if it wasn't poorly owned, if it's matchmaking didn't make it feel like a job a good portion of time rather than a game. We could of had Apex legends at it's fullest potential and Titanfall 3.

#简评
优点
1. 射击、机动手感顶尖，对局节奏快，进化护甲鼓励正面交战。
2. 英雄技能搭配玩法丰富，三人小队战术性强，Ping系统方便组队沟通。
3. 核心玩法免费，氪金不影响平衡，赛季持续更新内容。
4. 有复活机制，上手简单、操作上限高，单局耗时短。
缺点
1. 无国服，国内游玩需加速器，常出现卡顿、丢包。
2. PC端外挂、炸鱼问题依旧存在。
3. 英雄、排位平衡时有争议，部分皮肤定价偏高。
4. 后期新玩法创新不足，内容偏同质化。
总结
玩法质量顶尖的战术竞技FPS，但网络、外挂、运营是明显短板。如果你能接受小毛病，它仍是当前最好玩的 FPS 大逃杀，没有之一。

Apex Legends is one of the best battle royale games available today. It combines fast-paced action, unique characters, and strategic team gameplay. Each Legend has special abilities that make every match feel different and exciting. The movement system is smooth, the gunplay feels satisfying, and the ping system makes communication easy even without a microphone. The game is free to play and receives regular updates with new content, maps, and Legends. While it can be challenging for beginners, the gameplay is rewarding once you learn the mechanics. Overall, Apex Legends is an exciting and competitive shooter that offers hours of fun for both casual and serious players

Прикольно, но то еще говнище. Бан от клоунов РКН очень сладко накладываются на абсолютно убогие сервера, из-за чего играть становится еще противнее(это если вообще запустило в матч). В добавок идут бездарные и тупые тиммейты, которым еще и токсичности не занимать. Про баланс вообще молчу, идиоты бафают персонажа, когда на него выходит новенький скинчик стоимостью в отдельную хорошую игру, а в следующем сезоне его настолько сильно нерфят, что играть просто невозможно становится. Карты просто полный ужас, 75% дня мап пул полное говнище, состоящий из отстойных карт по типу олимпа и "Квартала ЭЫЭЫЭЫ", которые большинству уже просто невероятно надоели. А донат работает по отработанной схеме из сотен других подобных игр: покупается только визуал(единственное адекватное стоит ужасных денег). Да, его можно приобрести за "Металлы", но большинство из предоставленных приколов выглядит так плохо, что хочется выйти из игры, а все, что ниже легендарного выглядит еще хуже. И персонажи, которых либо ты покупаешь за свои кровные, либо сидишь сутками в игре, чтобы открыть одного, которого через день превратят в беспомощного инвалида. Да, раз в сезон тебе сбросят сотню монеток с барского стола, на которые можно купить кейсик по акционочке с которого выпадет самая отсталая анимация на бесполезного персонажа. А так побегать пострелять прикольно и весело

Tytuł: Symulator bycia kurierem sprzętu dla 14-letnich spocących się dzieciaków

Treść:
Odpalasz Apexa pełen nadziei na dobry mecz. Gra litościwie paruje cię z dwoma losowymi "pro-playerami". Pierwszy to oczywiście Wraith z dopiskiem TTV w nicku, która w ułamku sekundy odłącza się od oddziału w trakcie lotu, ląduje w najgęściej zaludnionym budynku na mapie, dostaje w ryj z pistoletu na wodę, po czym robi "disconnect" z prędkością światła – jeszcze zanim jej wirtualne ciało zdąży dotknąć ziemi.

Drugi kolega z drużyny to gość, który najwyraźniej gra na kierownicy od Pegasusa, a przez jego wiecznie włączony mikrofon słyszysz odkurzacz, szczekającego psa i awanturę rodzinną w nieznanym narzeczu.

Zostajesz sam. Postanawiasz grać taktycznie. Biegasz po pustkowiach przez 15 minut jak jakiś zbieracz złomu. Kolekcjonujesz fioletowe i złote dodatki, masz ulubioną broń, zapas apteczek i czerwony pancerz. Czujesz się jak chodzący czołg, gotowy wygrać mistrzostwa świata. Nagle słyszysz strzały i myślisz: "To jest ten moment".

Zbliżasz się do walki, ale zanim w ogóle zdążysz wcisnąć lewy przycisk myszy, z nieba spada na ciebie naszprycowany stymulantami Octane, a tuż za nim Horizon wykręcająca fikołki pod sufitem. Gra natychmiast zamienia się w festiwal "third-party", gdzie nagle z każdej strony magicznie pojawiają się cztery inne ekipy. Padasz w ułamku sekundy, uświadamiając sobie smutną prawdę: przez kwadrans grałeś po prostu w symulator kuriera DPD, żeby z największą starannością wyfarmić i dostarczyć swój cenny loot jakiemuś e-sportowemu napinaczowi, który nie widział trawy od premiery trzeciego sezonu.

Nie zapominajmy o wisience na torcie – serwerach EA napędzanych chomikiem w kołowrotku, przez które twój strzał ze strzelby prosto w klatkę piersiową przeciwnika z odległości pół metra zada potężne, niszczycielskie 9 punktów obrażeń.

10/10, doskonała produkcja dla osób, które muszą szybko i skutecznie podnieść sobie ciśnienie bez użycia farmakologii.

《Apex英雄》是一款玩法出众但问题突出的战术竞技FPS，依托扎实的射击手感、丰富的英雄技能与立体身法体系，搭配复活机制、智能标记等人性化设计，战术深度和对局乐趣拉满，电竞生态也十分成熟；但PC端外挂泛滥、服务器不稳、匹配机制失衡、入门门槛高，再加上氪金与BUG等问题，大幅影响游玩体验，适合喜欢硬核竞技、能接受短板且有队友开黑的玩家，休闲玩家慎入。
除了操控手感以外全是一坨的游戏，垃圾到死的排位，永远爆炸的服务器，所有游戏里最牛逼的挂哥，这游戏现在还留着的老逼全都是为了捞薯条上号的，居然还有人推荐新人入坑，我祝他全家爆炸

我先承认，十分羞愧，我的游戏时长断断续续加起来只有不到100小时，但我还是想说两句
实话说，今年我高一，我在初中时玩过，但是当时我的技术太差了（我本来就比较习惯我的世界或者边缘世界那种比较休闲的游戏，不太喜欢射击更喜欢魂系近战），遇到人甚至都反应不过来那是人。但这一两年玩了塔科夫、三角洲、CS1.6等等游戏，枪法和脑子核反应上来之后玩APEX，体验不是一般的好。
它确实存在一些问题，外挂、土豆服务器、上手门槛高等等，但是只要尽可能地看淡，或者说是放平心态，像我一样一遍又一遍重新匹配，不断地死，终究会看见自己的提升。曾经我是真的一个人都打不到。但现在也能偶尔拿一两次捍卫者。
我真的很喜欢这款游戏，可能有人会说我是M，没被虐够，但是我就是很佛系的，玩就得了，就是打排位会注意认真点。
上手永远是最难的，塔科夫、钢4、僵毁，一堆游戏上手都是很难的，但是坚持，不断尝试，就过去了
以及最重要的，尽管我是个菜逼，甚至算是半个新人，但我认识到一件事情就是！不要压力！，给新人留一点空间，很一部分人（就排除掉射击游戏很好或者经常打别的到了APEX也照样NB的）都是从枪线都跟不上人摸过来的，匹配机制是什么样子的大家都有目共睹，新手刚完成教程开一把匹配都能遇见击杀几千几百的大佬，实力分布线常年贴在范围最低都是有的。都是从新手走过来，不要赶走新鲜血液才是让这个游戏能持续活着的重中之重，要去炸鱼去打排位也行啊（至少我听说排位就是炸鱼和被炸，赌的不是有没有挂而是这把有几队挂）.
总之——我很菜，我在努力，我确实在变强

1. El Impacto de la Hipermovilidad y el "Power Creep"
Lo bueno y lo malo del ritmo actual: Es verdad que la movilidad ha evolucionado a un nivel frenético. Esto hace que las partidas sean increíblemente intensas y rápidas (lo cual es muy divertido), pero tiene un efecto colateral: la obsolescencia de los personajes clásicos.

El problema: Leyendas originales o más antiguas se han quedado atrás ante el "Power Creep" (personajes nuevos con habilidades mucho más completas y móviles). Si una leyenda no tiene una vía de escape vertical u horizontal o un posicionamiento ultra veloz, le cuesta muchísimo encajar en el meta actual.

Propuesta constructiva: En lugar de solo lanzar personajes nuevos, Respawn debería implementar un plan agresivo de reworks (rediseños) para las leyendas antiguas, actualizando sus kits para que puedan competir en igualdad de condiciones en este Apex moderno y ultra veloz.

2. La Barrera de Entrada: Desbloqueo de Leyendas demasiado lento
El problema: Aunque el juego es Free-to-Play, la economía para los jugadores nuevos o casuales es agotadora. Conseguir las 12,000 fichas de leyenda necesarias para desbloquear un solo personaje requiere demasiadas horas de juego. Con un plantel que ya supera las 25 leyendas, para alguien que empieza hoy, la meta de tenerlos a todos se siente inalcanzable y frustrante.

Propuesta constructiva:

Reducir el costo en fichas de las leyendas más antiguas (por ejemplo, que las de las primeras temporadas cuesten la mitad).

Introducir desafíos semanales específicos que recompensen directamente con fichas de leyenda o que permitan probar personajes bloqueados durante el fin de semana.

3. El Emparejamiento (Matchmaking) Roto
El problema: Esto es, sin duda, lo más "horrible" y dañino para la retención de jugadores. Que el sistema junte a un nivel 5 (que apenas está aprendiendo a apuntar y a deslizarse) en la misma partida con un Apex Predator (el top 0.1% del mundo) rompe cualquier lógica de progresión justa. No es divertido para el novato, que muere sin entender qué pasó, ni tiene sentido competitivo.

Propuesta constructiva: Implementar un "muro de contención" estricto para niveles bajos (por ejemplo, del nivel 1 al 50). Los jugadores nuevos deberían emparejarse únicamente entre ellos y con bots de entrenamiento en partidas públicas hasta que dominen las mecánicas básicas, protegiéndolos por completo de las salas de alto nivel.

This game IS NOT BEGINNER FRIENDLY! As a low-level player, the algorithm constantly throws me into lobbies with literal Apex Predators. The "Engagement-Optimized Matchmaking" (EOMM) will manipulate your games, giving you one easy lobby followed by ten matches where you get absolutely crushed by sweaty three-stacks. 自2019年横空出世以来，《Apex Legends》凭借现象级的热度彻底改写了大逃杀赛道的固有格局。依托《泰坦陨落》世界观与引擎基底，Respawn跳出传统“跳伞搜装、苟活吃鸡”的同质化框架，将英雄技能体系、高速机动玩法与战术竞技深度融合，打造出一款快节奏、高自由、重配合的创新型大逃杀作品。在无数吃鸡游戏迭代消亡的浪潮中，Apex始终保持稳定热度，凭借独树一帜的游玩体验、持续优化的内容生态与成熟的竞技体系，成为当下兼顾娱乐性与竞技性的标杆级战术射击网游。
一、核心定位：英雄吃鸡，打破大逃杀的玩法桎梏
传统大逃杀游戏普遍依赖纯枪法与运气，玩法模式固化、战术单一，而《Apex Legends》精准找准品类突破口，构建了战术技能+高速机动+团队协作的全新大逃杀体系。游戏沿用经典60人跳伞缩圈的吃鸡核心规则，主打3人小队竞技，同时创新性引入英雄职业系统，彻底摆脱“人人属性相同”的传统设定，让每一局对抗都兼具枪法博弈与战术策略。
相较于PUBG等写实向慢节奏吃鸡，Apex的核心定位清晰且独特：它不追求极致拟真，而是主打轻量化、高流畅、高对抗的竞技体验。没有繁琐的配件堆叠、没有拖沓的搜装流程，地图紧凑、物资密集、转移灵活，每一局节奏高效不拖沓。同时游戏延续零数值付费的公平竞技底线，所有英雄技能、对局机制免费解锁，付费内容仅为外观皮肤，完美平衡休闲娱乐与硬核竞技，适配全层级玩家需求。
二、玩法体验：机动拉满，战术与枪法的多维博弈
1. 英雄体系：职业分化，构筑多元战术上限
英雄系统是《Apex Legends》的核心灵魂，也是区别于所有传统吃鸡游戏的核心标识。游戏拥有数十位人设、技能、定位截然不同的传奇英雄，分为突击、侦察、支援、控制四大职业，每位英雄均配备专属被动、战术技能与终极大招，技能设计贴合角色定位，无同质化模板套路。
突击英雄擅长突破拉扯、正面攻坚；侦察英雄可探测敌人信息、掌控战场视野；支援英雄主打续航抬人、团队兜底；控制英雄能够分割战场、封锁走位。小队搭配自由度极高，可自由组合攻防、拉扯、续航体系，衍生出无数战术打法。无论是路人局的灵活冲脸，还是职业赛场的精密运营，英雄技能的联动配合都能打出极强的战术效果，彻底打破了传统吃鸡“唯枪法论”的单一博弈模式，大幅拉高了游戏的战术深度。
2. 极致机动：告别笨重，重塑战场转移逻辑
继承《泰坦陨落》的机动基因，Apex拥有目前大逃杀品类中最顶尖的移动手感，彻底告别传统吃鸡的笨重走位。滑铲、蹬墙跳、高空滑翔、快速贴墙转移等轻量化机动操作，让玩家的战场走位极度灵活。无论是落地抢点、遇敌拉扯、残血撤退，还是决赛圈绕后突袭，丰富的机动手段都能让战术选择更加多元。
高机动机制带来的核心体验，是对局容错率大幅提升。玩家不再局限于定点架枪、被动对峙，可通过灵活走位规避伤害、拉扯战场节奏，新手不会因枪法劣势直接被碾压，老手则能凭借身法与操作打出极致拉扯，让每一场对战都充满动态博弈的乐趣，彻底杜绝了传统吃鸡“落地成盒、全程苟分”的枯燥体验。
3. 枪械与对局机制：流畅爽快，兼顾平衡与深度
游戏的枪械体系设计成熟均衡，手感流畅扎实，弹道反馈清晰易懂，兼顾新手上手与核心玩家钻研空间。武器分类清晰，步枪、冲锋枪、霰弹枪、狙击枪、轻机枪各司其职，适配近、中、远全距离作战场景。枪械后坐力可控性强，没有过于玄学的弹道散布，新手稍加练习即可掌握基础压枪逻辑，同时高阶的跟枪、急停、身法对枪存在足够操作上限。
值得一提的是Apex独特的长TTK机制，相较于CS:GO等短TTK一击秒杀的硬核对局，Apex角色坦度更高、对战过程更长。这一设计让对局更注重技能配合、走位拉扯与团队支援，而非单纯的枪法秒杀，极大降低了新手的瞬时挫败感，同时让团战的战术博弈过程更饱满、更有层次感。
此外，游戏独创的重生机制、队友标记系统、智能物资提示，大幅降低了团队沟通门槛。即便路人无语音交流，也能通过精准的标记系统完成报点、物资需求、战术沟通，残血队友可通过重生信标复活，避免一局失误直接出局的尴尬，大幅提升了对局的可玩性与容错率。
三、视听与世界观：科幻硬核，氛围感与辨识度兼备
依托《泰坦陨落》正统世界观，《Apex Legends》构建了独树一帜的科幻硬核星际竞技风格，世界观饱满且逻辑自洽。各大英雄的人设背景相互串联，赛事竞技、星际争夺的核心设定清晰，让每一场对局不再是单纯的对抗，而是拥有完整世界观支撑的竞技赛事，代入感十足。
视听表现上，游戏画面风格干净利落，场景建模精细，科幻风地图设计层次感丰富，从荒漠峡谷到都市空间站，每一张地图都拥有独特的地形地貌与场景特色，高低差、掩体、通道设计合理，完美适配高速机动与战术对抗。技能特效华丽但不杂乱，配色克制，不会出现特效遮挡视野的问题，兼顾视觉效果与竞技实用性。
音效设计同样表现出色，枪械射击、技能释放、脚步声、位移声分层清晰、辨识度极高，既能通过音效反馈提升对战沉浸感，也能帮助玩家听声辨位、判断战场局势。对局节奏紧凑，BGM贴合竞技氛围，全程不喧宾夺主，专注服务对局体验。
四、现存短板：迭代争议与长期痛点
尽管综合表现优异，但历经多年迭代，《Apex Legends》依然存在诸多难以忽视的短板，制约着游戏的大众普及度与长期体验。
首先是版本数值波动大，平衡性不稳定。游戏每赛季都会对英雄、枪械、配件进行大幅调整，数值起伏极其明显。强势英雄与枪械长期垄断对局环境，版本答案固化，冷门武器与英雄出场率低迷，玩家辛苦打磨的玩法体系、出装思路，很容易被版本更新直接淘汰，玩法沉淀难度较高。
其次是优化与BUG问题频发。作为一款长线运营游戏，Apex始终未能彻底解决卡顿、掉帧、模型加载异常、技能BUG等问题，新赛季更新往往伴随全新BUG，部分英雄技能、枪械机制偶尔出现异常失效、判定错误等情况，严重影响对局体验。同时游戏配置优化参差不齐，中低配设备难以稳定满帧运行，画面卡顿、延迟波动问题频发。
此外，入门门槛模糊，新手体验两极分化。相较于硬核的CS:GO，Apex上手难度更低，但融合了身法、技能、机动、战术多重机制，想要精通难度极高。新手容易陷入“会开枪、不会拉扯、不会配合技能”的瓶颈，同时路人局炸鱼、车队碾压现象普遍，单排玩家游戏体验不稳定，队友配合断层极易导致对局崩盘。最后，赛季通行证内容同质化严重，付费皮肤溢价较高，性价比偏低，也是玩家长期诟病的问题。
五、整体总结与适配人群
综合来看，《Apex Legends》是一款创新度拉满、娱乐与竞技兼具的顶级大逃杀作品。它打破了传统吃鸡的玩法僵局，以高机动对战、多元英雄战术、流畅的对局节奏、公平的竞技生态，构建了独一无二的战术射击体系。兼顾休闲玩家的娱乐需求与核心玩家的竞技追求，容错率适中、博弈层次丰富，是目前综合体验最均衡的大逃杀网游。
但版本平衡性波动、优化短板、进阶门槛偏高的问题，让它难以做到全年龄段、全玩家群体适配，依然存在明显的体验上限。
适配人群：喜欢快节奏吃鸡、厌倦传统苟分流玩法、偏爱技能战术配合与身法拉扯的射击玩家，适合喜欢团队竞技、追求多元对局乐趣、想要兼顾娱乐与冲分的玩家。
慎入人群：偏爱写实拟真硬核射击、习惯慢节奏运营、讨厌版本频繁变动、追求极致稳定游戏体验的玩家，不建议入坑。
六、最终评分：8.3/10
玩法创新 9.2：重构大逃杀玩法体系，英雄技能+高机动拉扯打破品类桎梏，战术自由度与对局新鲜感拉满
视听表现 8.5：科幻世界观饱满，画面干净适配竞技，音效分层清晰，整体辨识度与沉浸感优异
游戏深度 8.8：枪法、身法、技能、团队战术多维博弈，上手易、精通难，对局上限高，耐玩性极强
新手友好 7.0：入门门槛低，容错率高，但进阶难度大，路人局环境不稳定，新手成长路径较模糊
版本稳定 7.2：内容更新积极，但数值波动大、BUG频发，优化与平衡性问题长期存在
总体而言，《Apex Legends》重新定义了现代大逃杀游戏的形态。它没有陷入传统吃鸡的同质化内卷，而是用机动与战术赋予品类全新生命力。既有轻松娱乐的休闲对局，也有深度博弈的竞技赛场，历经多年迭代依旧热度不减。在吃鸡品类整体疲软的当下，Apex凭借独特的玩法魅力，成为当之无愧的战术竞技标杆，也是所有大逃杀爱好者的首选作品之一。

您好，不管是谁看到这篇信件。我希望您能看完这些字。我游玩这个游戏已经3500个小时，2800小时来自我自己的账号。还有800小时另一个EA账号。我并不知道这个数字对于其他人来说有什么意义。但是对于马上22岁的我来说，这意味着我2%的人生的时间我都花在了名为Apex Legends的游戏中。我在人生活最黑暗的时间遇见了它。在那些因为看不见前途而难以入眠的夜晚，我在它带来的多巴胺褪去时睡去。我开始了解硬件设备，开始了解如何瞄准，开始重新要求自己变得更好。在那些被诟病的赛季里，我依然能感受到乐趣。它帮我交到朋友，帮我驱散作业的劳累，帮我找到每个早上和朋友的谈资。它并不是一款完美的游戏，有很多人说它太难，不友好，太无聊，lagging等等各种问题。但是我爱它。当看到我被BAN的时候，疑惑，害怕，恐惧，失望，愤怒？我不知道我该怎么形容当时的感觉。我并不是在告别一个账号也不是一个游戏。而是我2%的人生。它们甚至不能作为回忆，它们是我这个人真正的一部分。我并不知道这个账号的最终结局会是怎么样子的。我是想告诉看见看见这段文字的人，我热爱这款游戏，或许很难继续下去了，但是2%的我永远留在了这里。

I honestly don't understand why there are so many negative reviews.

I'm primarily a CS and Dota player, and I've just started playing this game. After about 10 hours, I'm having an absolute blast. Not only am I enjoying it, but I'm also performing well, consistently getting plenty of kills and dealing solid damage.

To anyone considering trying this game: go for it!

Don't let the negative reviews discourage you. Instead of focusing on what others are saying, try it yourself and form your own opinion. A lot of the complaints seem to come from players who get frustrated when they struggle to understand how they died or adapt to the game's mechanics.

Competitive shooters aren't meant to be easy from the start. They require learning, practice, and patience. If you're willing to put in the time, there's a lot of fun to be had here.

I've already picked up the Ultimate+ Battle Pass, and I'm really excited to unlock Ranked mode and start climbing the ladder. Looking forward to seeing how far I can go!

Holy crap is this game fun
And holy crap is this game [i]difficult[/i].
If you are trying to play this as a beginner, it's going to take a LOT of time to learn to even stand in the same ring as late-game players. If you're casual, unranked is definitely a game you can have fun in. But if you're the type to GRIND, this game is your ideal playground.

There are tons of legends you can pick from and the abilities they have are so fun to learn and they all have different uses, there are also even classes and specific perks that change how your legends abilities work. Anyways, lets get into the pros and cons.

[b]Pros[/b]
[*]Fast-paced game with a lot of strategy
[*]Several legends to pick from with their own unique abilities
[*]Guns are smooth to use and there's tons of variety
[*]Very good optimizations for my nearly 10 year old untouched PC
[*]HIGH skill ceiling. Lots of movement tech for you to learn and to master. But you don't also need to be incredibly good to play good, it's all in the strategy either way. But for those who'd want to reach predator, everything is available for you to learn, and it is intensily detailed.

[b]Cons[/b]
[*]GRINDSLOP. You need to play so many hours in order to unlock a legend. That can be a turn off. I have 242 hours and I've unlocked 10, 6 of them is included as your starting roster. That means in the 242 hours I've played, I've only unlocked 4, and there is 17 remaining. 
[*]High skill ceiling. Yes I put it here as well, to be honest it's a lot. Sometimes I wanna chill and play the game but I get blasted to oblivion by incredibly good players who can tap strafe and wall jump and then slide mantle jump onto rope cancel rope jump wattson fence slow mozambique kill. Aight I got the memo bro.
[*] Grindslop. Did I say that already?
[*] Time to kill is extremely fast. You can be lasered in one second and die, which is a thing you learn early on. All it takes is one second for you to die if you play out of position or not paying attention. You need to learn positioning and a bit of basic movement tech to be able to survive the first initial burst of damage. It comes with the grind.
[*] Weapons usually have a meta. Most games when you're above plat people start to use Hemlok and something else and there's not big variety. It's just "this weapon is good so use it rest of the game".

It's a free game, I'm not gonna ramble so play it, try it, find the fun guns, the fun legends and learn some basic beginner movements, and you'll understand why it's so addicting. The moment you manage to slide away from shooting opponents, get a shield up and then kill the rushing enemy team is when you'll get hooked on it.

Не знаю, с чего начать
Для меня это больше чем игра, она связана для меня со школьным временем. Очень хорошо помню, как перед новым годом у меня было очень плохое настроение, но тут мне звонит дружище с которым я играю в апекс, и говорит что там добавили новый новогодний ивент с новым режимом(легендарный поезд на Краю Света).
И это было восхитительно. Я играл в неё в общей сложности наверное лет 5, с момента середины первого сезона. Наиграл, наверное, пару тысяч часов. Это всё было на ПС4, потому тут у меня 8 часов. 
И вот я попробовал в неё вернуться вновь после того как я отслужил в армии, а бро улетел в Китай, из за чего вместе поиграть нам теперь очень сложно. Недавно я в неё играл с лучшим другом, мы вспоминали былое время, которое казалось как оно часто бывает - раньше трава была зеленее и прочее. 
Субъективно, сейчас игра кажется немного перегруженной - у каждого персонажа свой класс, свои особенности, свои собственные мини-перки которые ты получаешь по мере катки. Это прикольно и добавляет глубины, но мне, человеку наигравшему тысячи часов в сие творение, было тяжело разобраться в новых особенностях.
Но несмотря на старания текущей команды респавн или кто там сейчас у руля, онлайн в игре кажется падает. В ру сегменте уж точно. В дуо режиме половина карты уже ограничена, а вместо обычных (вроде) 45 игроков сейчас 30. И это очень грустно
Вкатываться в Арех сейчас - сложно, однако как баттл рояль игра всё еще очень даже хороша. Новичкам - рекомендую. Старичкам - решайте сами. Готовы ли увидеть, что стало с игрой, некогда занимающей особое место в вашем сердце?

1) Проблемы с серверами - обходы каждый раз ломаются, устаёшь менять впны, прокси и все типы обходов, чтобы поиграть

⊹ ࣪ ˖ **Recommended** ˖ ࣪ ⊹

**Apex Legends™** is a game I do not really play anymore, but I did enjoy it a lot when I was playing. It gave me some fun memories, even if it is not something I come back to much now. ⋆˚꩜｡

The gameplay is fast, chaotic, and very team-focused. You pick from different Legends, each with their own abilities, drop into matches, loot weapons and gear, fight other squads, and try to be the last team standing. ✧

I liked how each character brought something different to the game. The abilities made fights feel more interesting, and the movement made everything feel quick and intense. When a match was going well, it felt really satisfying. ˚₊‧꒰ა ☆ ໒꒱ ‧₊˚

It can definitely be stressful, especially if you are not playing with the right team or if the matchmaking feels rough, but when it is fun, it is really fun. ⟡

Overall, **Apex Legends™** is a solid battle royale with fun characters, fast gameplay, and a lot of chaotic moments. I may not play it anymore, but I enjoyed the time I spent with it. ✨

Apex Legends is a philisophical question posed as a first person battle royale. On its surface, it appears as a free to play game made by a game studio riding the coatails of a revolutionary and fantastic game series. After its veneer of "product of its time" is peeled away, all that stands is a tough question. 

How much disrespect are you willing to endure? This is the question at the heart of this game. 

Apex Legends pillages and tears at the carcass of Titanfall, drip-feeding its lifeblood to its players. Long time fans of Titanfall will reminice on the familiar guns and memorabilia of the two predeceossors of Apex Legends. It gives you enough of a sense of care for the franchise that had built the foundation Apex sits on, however never enough to satisfy. Like a carrot leading each and every player to the never ending day that something new (or God foreake something better) will come out of the hellish bowels of EA and Respawn entertainment. 

How much disrespect are you willing to endure?

Are you willing to endure a matchmaking system so bipolar that average players will be matched with the top 500 most skilled or often times, carried players? Contsantly being placed in (un-ranked) sweatfests where the most fun you can attain is by how ridiculous you can contort your character model to dodge the enslaught of bullets heading directly to you. 

Are you willing to endure servers so poor that bullets will ignore the existence of its brethren code that is collision to any object that isint your character? This, keep in mind, is compounded with its host servers notorious for instability and sluggishness.

Are you willing to endure characters so unlikable and downright unbalanced that their mere existence is a blight to be witnessed anytime their assembly of pixels is projected unto your eyes?

Are you willing to endure the predatory microtransaction scheme that constantly re-invents ways to flush your money into the pockets of billionare company EA?

And are you willing to endure the most widely abused system that plauges crossplay in the shooter genre? At higher-than-average level of play, in my limited dataset, 90% of PC players choose to forgoe their more accurate pheriperal in-place of an artificially enhanced "ez trak" system. This enforces an awful mindset of join or be out-gunned at close ranges. When losing to players that choose to play on controller to gain a buff to their tracking feels less like being outskilled and more like slighted by a game that dosent respect a player content with making miniscule mistakes. 

How much disrespect are you willing to endure?

If you are a proponent of teaching yourself to reinforce your mental fortitude and composure when being treated as nothing but a useful wallet. Then the answer is the the above questions is I will endure. 

As for my friends and I, I will say that we will endure this product as the suffering it throws is puny in comparison to what Escape From Tarkov has already thrown our way.

TLDR;

7/10 game, is alright, kinda sweaty.

I love Apex. Now my hours are low on Steam. However, I have played mostly on console and haven't fully crossed over to a PC to play it yet. This game is amazing and should not be slept on.  Apex is a free-to-play, squad-based battle Royale hero shooter.  Each character has a distinct personality, a passive trait, a tactical ability and an ultimate ability. All playable characters (Legends) are divided into five specific classes: Assault, Skirmisher, Recon, Controller and Support. Apex is a battle Royale format: Squads of three (or two) are dropped onto a large island map. Your team must scavenge for weapons, shields, and supplies while fighting off other squads until only one team remains. The map doesn't always have squads and duos. It rotates. However, trios are always there (wish all the options were there all the time). The game has a practice range to learn the guns and items in the game, alongside bots to practice aiming at buildings to get a feel for the game. They have other game modes that help me with my game, like gun run, control and more. There is a comp, but I have never dared lol. I would say apex is more fun with friends. I can play it alone, but for me so much better with friends.

You will love the game, you will hate the game, but you can't convince me there's another FPS that is as optimized as this, to the point that I can play it on a college laptop, have fun, and still be able to say I hate how greedy it is, BUT YOU CAN'T DENY LOBA

I have 2500 hours in apex on steam and about 2000 hours on other platforms and other accounts. I really like this game! But I agree that the quality and quantity of unique content in apex has decreased significantly over the years. For example, Alter was released in season 21. The next hero, Axel, was released in season 29. A season lasts an average of 3 months, which means we haven't seen a new hero for 24 months. 2 years!!!! I'm very glad that the developers still care about the servers (haha, servers go brrrr-time out), they make rebalances, change maps sometimes, but it's still not enough to attract new players. As a result, only super-loyal players who have been playing the game for years remain in the game. The average skill in the game is increasing, which means that it's less interesting for new players to play even at lower ranks. This further reduces the influx of loyal players. The game could potentially become a second heroes of the storm. I hope the Respawn team can break this vicious cycle. Because we love the apex very much!
Also, I really hope that the developers won't make Apex Legends 2, but will continue to work on their main product, as it is conceptually endless. Maybe it's worth continuing the story of the Titanfall world, rather than creating new skins for $300 and knives that glow brightly in the dark?
Here are some ideas:
They could create something like new 5v5 arenas to take a piece of the CS2 and Valorant players. 
It is possible to return a permanent solo mode, where only bald Wraiths with a kunai will play. 
If you use old developments, you can also return the train, but make it on a new map like Storm Point, such as Beach Express, and make three new colorful skins for Axel, Conduit, and Maggie. 
Or you can create a new map, such as "European Forest" with fir trees, oaks, and change the game rules from "open space for Axel's maneuvers" to "hiding behind a tree trunk," adding a new character with the ability to create a reflection to hide behind it, for example.

Juego bastante a los Battle Royale y uno de los que más horas le metí fue Apex Legends. Entre Steam y Origin debo tener unas 300 horas aproximadamente, y sinceramente puedo decir que fue uno de los juegos que más me costó aprender a jugar “bien”.

A diferencia de otros Battle Royale como Warzone, PUBG o incluso Counter cuando hablamos de shooters competitivos, Apex tiene una movilidad y un gameplay muchísimo más rápido y complicado. El tema de las habilidades, el movimiento, el tiempo para matar y las peleas súper frenéticas hacen que sea bastante difícil dominarlo. De hecho, es de esos juegos que, aunque le metas muchísimas horas, sentís que nunca terminás de aprender del todo. Personalmente es uno de los shooters que jamás pude llegar a dominar aunque sea un poco, y justamente eso también hace que tenga algo especial.

Algo que sí siento es que Apex es un juego prácticamente hecho para jugar con amigos. Obviamente podés jugar solo, pero la experiencia cambia muchísimo cuando entrás con squad y buena comunicación. No es como CS, PUBG o Warzone donde capaz podés carrear más individualmente o jugar tranquilo solo; acá el trabajo en equipo y coordinar habilidades es demasiado importante.

Dentro de los Battle Royale, Apex sigue siendo bastante único comparado a los demás. La movilidad, los personajes y el ritmo de las partidas hacen que se sienta totalmente distinto. Pero también tiene varios puntos negativos. Uno bastante molesto es el hecho de no poder jugar realmente solo dentro del modo principal, porque dependés muchísimo del squad y muchas veces te terminan tocando compañeros que ni hablan o se van apenas los matan.

Y otro problema grande es que hoy en día la mayoría de jugadores son demasiado buenos. Se nota muchísimo que ya casi no hay jugadores nuevos, entonces muchas partidas terminan siendo bastante complicadas incluso para gente que ya tiene horas encima. A veces parece que todos juegan como si estuvieran en ranked competitivo y eso puede hacer que aprender o simplemente relajarse jugando sea medio frustrante.

Aun así, Apex me parece un juegazo y probablemente uno de los Battle Royale más originales que salieron en los últimos años. Tiene una jugabilidad increíble, peleas súper intensas y momentos que posta son muy divertidos cuando jugás con amigos. Es difícil, sí, pero justamente eso también hace que cuando una partida sale bien se sienta muchísimo más satisfactoria.

Nota: 7/10.

Smooth, stays current, releases new champs regularly, balanced. All you could ask for in a battle royale.
